sources.notes: Kandoth et al. 2013 Nature TCGA Pan-Cancer paper across 12 cancer types, 127 SMGs total. PMC full text has gene symbols stripped from HTML (appear as empty references in JATS XML), so direct extraction is limited. Gene list assembled from: (1) text explicitly names KRAS Gly12/Gly13 codons, IDH1 Arg132, IDH2 Arg172 mutations; (2) abstract explicitly names TP53, PIK3CA, PTEN, APC, VHL, FBXW7, BAP1, GATA3, CDH1, DNMT3A, NPM1, PIK3R1; (3) Results describe cancer-type-specific drivers with mutation frequencies: KIRC=VHL(52%)+BAP1(33%)+PBRM1+SETD2, UCEC=ARID1A+CTNNB1+PIK3CA+PIK3R1+PTEN, AML=DNMT3A+NPM1+FLT3+IDH1/IDH2+RUNX1+TET2+CEBPA, BRCA=CDH1+MAP3K1+GATA3+TP53, GBM=IDH1+EGFR+NF1+BRAF, COAD/READ=APC+KRAS+CTNNB1+RNF43, LUAD=KRAS+EGFR+STK11+KEAP1+NFE2L2+NF1, LUSC=NFE2L2+KEAP1+FGFR3, BLCA=FGFR3+KDM6A, HNSC=NOTCH1+CASP8+FAT1, OV=TP53+BRCA1+BRCA2+NF1+RB1+CDK12; (4) SMG categories named in text: histone modifiers (KDM6A, KMT2C, KMT2D, SETD2), splicing (SF3B1, SRSF2, U2AF1), PI3K signaling (PIK3CA, PIK3R1, PTEN, AKT1, MTOR), Wnt/beta-catenin (CTNNB1, APC, RNF43), cell cycle (RB1, CDKN2A, CDKN1B). Excluded: genes mentioned only in Methods, background mutation rate models, or referenced papers.
